content:
{
  "title": "Translingual Bridge Prompting: Leveraging Pivot Languages for Low-Resource LLM Tasks",
  "problem": "Large language models (LLMs) often underperform on low-resource languages due to limited training data and transfer inefficiencies. Current prompting approaches rarely leverage interlingual relationships to enhance multilingual task accuracy.",
  "motivation": "Bridging knowledge from high-resource to low-resource languages via carefully designed prompts could mitigate data scarcity issues. Advances in multilingual LLMs and the availability of aligned corpora enable experimentation with cross-lingual transfer techniques. Improving performance on low-resource languages is critical for equitable global NLP applications.",
  "method": "We propose a novel 'Translingual Bridge Prompting' technique, wherein prompts explicitly incorporate a pivot (high-resource) language to mediate between the source (low-resource) and the task. The method creates composite prompts including context or exemplars in both the low-resource and a typologically/lexically similar high-resource language, encouraging the LLM to leverage shared knowledge. Prompt templates guide the model to solve the task first in the pivot language, then transfer or translate the reasoning to the low-resource target. Dynamic selection of pivot languages based on similarity metrics is integrated to maximize transfer benefits.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on multilingual QA and NLI datasets (e.g., XQuAD, FLORES, AmericasNLI), focusing on low-resource language subsets. Baselines include standard prompting and translation-based prompting. Metrics are accuracy and F1. Ablations will analyze the impact of different pivot language choices and prompt compositions."
}
